course.title: Learn Python
course.description: Master Python from scratch with hands-on interactive coding lessons. From variables to machine learning.

chapter.title: 6. Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
chapter.description: Model real-world concepts using Classes and Objects in Python.

lessons.title: Classes and Objects
objectives: Define a class using the class keyword
lessons.explanation: Python is an object-oriented programming language. Almost everything in Python is an object, with its properties and methods. A Class is like an object constructor, or a 'blueprint' for creating objects. You define a class using the `class` keyword.
lessons.analogy: A Class is like an architectural blueprint for a house. An Object is the actual physical house built from that blueprint. You can build many houses (objects) from the same blueprint (class).
common_mistakes: Forgetting the `self` parameter in class methods.
Confusing class variables with instance variables.
best_practices: Use PascalCase (capitalizing the first letter of each word) for Class names.
lessons.summary: Classes allow you to group data and functions together into single units, making large programs much easier to manage.

examples.title: Creating a Dog Class
examples.explanation: We create a Dog class with an initialization method `__init__` that sets the name. The `bark` method uses that name. Then we create an instance of Dog named 'Buddy'.

exercises.statement: Create a class `Cat` with an `__init__` method that accepts a `name`. Create a method `meow` that prints 'Meow!'. Instantiate a Cat named 'Whiskers' and call its meow method.
exercises.starter_code:
# Define Cat class


# Instantiate and call meow
exercises.hint: Remember to include `self` as the first parameter in both __init__ and meow.
